quoteFirst: There is only one thing to be said in favour of detention on a fine summer’s afternoon, and that is that it is very pleasant to come out of. The sun never seems so bright or the turf so green as during the first
quoteLast: has come out of the detention-room. One feels as if one were entering a new and very delightful world. There is also a touch of the Rip van Winkle feeling.
title: Mike
author: Wodehouse, P. G. (Pelham Grenville), 1881-1975

quoteFirst: PACIFIC DIVISION. Trains leave Tacoma at 7.30 a.m. and 6.30 p.m. daily. Arrive at Portland at 2.30 p.m. and
quoteLast: The 7.30 a.m. train makes connections with O. R. & N. train and O. & C. train. Trains leave Portland for Tacoma at 2.00 a.m. and 11.05 a.m. daily. Arrive at Tacoma at 8.35 a.m. and 6.30 p.m., connecting with O. R. & N.
title: Tacoma and Vicinity
author: Oakland Land, Loan and Trust Company

quoteFirst: The second experiment demanded from her four times three letters. Of these twelve letters, six were right at the first guess and
quoteLast: or two wrong trials. Taking only this little list of card and letter experiments together, we can say that the probabilities are only one to many billions that such a result would ever come by chance.
title: Psychology and Social Sanity
author: Münsterberg, Hugo, 1863-1916

quoteFirst: It was about one o'clock in the afternoon when the _Kicker_ appeared on Dry Bottom's street. At about
quoteLast: , Potter left the front of the office and walked to the rear room where he halted at the imposing stone. There he proceeded to "take down" the four forms. This done he calmly began distributing type.
title: The Coming of the Law
author: Seltzer, Charles Alden, 1875-1942; Amick, Robert Wesley, 1879-1969 [Illustrator]

quoteLast: as he dropped Doctor Brainard at the Surgical, where he roomed. He was just driving off when Miss Jacobs hurried out of the entrance.
title: Leerie
author: Sawyer, Ruth, 1880-1970; Balmer, Clinton, 1879-1967 [Illustrator]

quoteLast: when the gunboats Baron DeKalb, Cincinnati, and Louisville, all iron-clads, steamed up to within about three hundred yards of the fort, and opened fire upon it. As soon as the gunboats hove in sight, and before they fired a shot, the fort opened on them.
title: Pictorial history of the war for the Union, volume 2 (of 2)
author: Stephens, Ann S. (Ann Sophia), 1810-1886

quoteFirst: Not quite liking the look of the snow, Gardiner asked us to hold tight whilst he tried it. Finding it all right he kicked steps up, and at
quoteLast: we reached the cairn, having taken one hour and thirty-five minutes from the glacier. The great wall rose straight above us, but the way up, which we had had no difficulty in making out with the telescope from below, was no longer to be seen.
title: True Tales of Mountain Adventures: For Non-Climbers Young and Old
author: Le Blond, Aubrey, Mrs., 1861-1934
